digest.top_line: STAMPEDE M0: adding abiraterone to RT+ADT in high-risk non-met prostate cuts MFS (HR 0.53, 6yr 82% vs 69%), OS HR 0.60; enzalutamide adds nothing.
digest.tldr: Prostate carried the day: STAMPEDE extends abiraterone's metastatic-HSPC OS benefit into high-risk M0, where 85% of pts receive definitive RT (74Gy/37fx), so this reads as systemic intensification on an RT+ADT backbone. Adding enzalutamide to abiraterone buys nothing (interaction HR 1.02, p=0.91).

sites.intro: Single card, but an RT-adjacent one: abiraterone added to the definitive RT+ADT backbone in high-risk M0.

studies.name: STAMPEDE (abiraterone ± enzalutamide, high-risk non-metastatic)
studies.tldr: MFS HR 0.53 (6yr 82% vs 69%) adding abiraterone ± enzalutamide to ADT in high-risk M0; OS HR 0.60.
details: 📊 Combination (abiraterone ± enzalutamide) vs ADT alone — comparison values omitted (cell value "0.53" not verified in source)
🔍 Pooled analysis of two STAMPEDE phase 3 RCTs, N=1974, open-label, 113 UK/Swiss sites, median f/u 72mo
🔍 High-risk M0: node+ or node− with ≥2 of T3/T4, Gleason 8-10, PSA≥40; or high-risk relapse; 39% node+
🔍 RT mandated for node− disease, encouraged for node+; 85% (1684/1974) planned to receive RT
🔍 RT dose 74Gy/37fx to prostate + seminal vesicles, or a hypofractionated equivalent
💊 Abiraterone 1000mg + prednisolone 5mg (± enzalutamide 160mg) ×2yr, added to 3yr ADT
📐 Adding enzalutamide to abiraterone gave no extra MFS: interaction HR 1.02 (0.70-1.50), p=0.91
🔗 Extends abiraterone's established OS benefit from metastatic HSPC (LATITUDE, STAMPEDE-M1) into high-risk M0
⚠️ Open-label and MFS is a surrogate, but the concordant OS benefit blunts both concerns

verdict.rationale: Randomised phase 3 evidence, prespecified MFS primary hit with concordant OS at 72-mo f/u, applicable high-risk M0 population on standard RT+ADT; open-label but objective endpoints.
verdict.audience: High-risk non-metastatic prostate cancer, on definitive RT + long-term ADT

studies.significance: The RT read: 85% (1684/1974) were on definitive RT plus long-term ADT, so this is the intensification decision for the reader's own high-risk M0 patient. Abiraterone alone suffices; adding enzalutamide gave no extra MFS (interaction HR 1.02). RT was standard 74Gy/37fx, so the result transfers to routine practice.
studies.monday_clinic: In high-risk M0 prostate cancer planned for definitive RT plus long-term ADT, this supports intensifying with 2 years of abiraterone; it does not extend to lower-risk M0 or to patients in whom RT is omitted.

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: Prospectively-pooled meta-analysis of two randomised phase 3 trials within the STAMPEDE platform protocol; open-label; 113 UK/Swiss sites; 1974 randomised; median follow-up 72 mo. Powered for MFS (target HR 0.75, ~315 control events, one-sided α 1.25%).

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: High-risk non-metastatic prostate cancer: node-positive, or node-negative with ≥2 of T3/T4, Gleason 8-10, PSA ≥40 ng/mL; or high-risk relapse. Median age 68, median PSA 34 ng/mL, 39% node-positive.

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: Local RT mandated for node-negative and encouraged for node-positive disease; 74 Gy/37 fx to prostate and seminal vesicles or a hypofractionated equivalent. 85% (1684/1974) planned to receive RT.

analysis_sections.label: Regimen
analysis_sections.body: Abiraterone acetate 1000 mg + prednisolone 5 mg daily, ± enzalutamide 160 mg daily in the second trial, for 2 years, atop 3 years of ADT. If RT was omitted, combination continued until progression.

analysis_sections.label: Endpoints
analysis_sections.body: Primary (meta-analysis): metastasis-free survival. Secondary: overall survival, prostate-cancer-specific survival, biochemical failure-free survival, PFS, toxicity.

analysis_sections.label: Results
analysis_sections.body: MFS HR 0.53 (0.44-0.64), p<0.0001; 6-yr MFS 82% vs 69% (180 vs 306 events). OS HR 0.60 (0.48-0.73), p<0.0001. Adding enzalutamide to abiraterone gave no extra benefit (interaction HR 1.02, p=0.91; I² p=0.90).

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: Mirrors abiraterone's OS benefit established in metastatic HSPC (LATITUDE, STAMPEDE-M1), now extended into high-risk non-metastatic disease. Enzalutamide added nothing incremental over abiraterone.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents high-risk M0 prostate cancer mostly managed with definitive RT + long-term ADT. Does not address lower-risk M0, metastatic disease, or the RT-omitted subset (where combination ran until progression).

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: Open-label; MFS is a surrogate, though the OS signal is concordant. Primary analysis is a pooled meta-analysis; prostate-cancer-specific survival and AE detail are truncated in the source excerpt.

open_questions: Which high-risk M0 subgroups need abiraterone vs ADT+RT alone

related_trials.brief_title: Treating Prostate Cancer That Has Come Back After Surgery With Apalutamide and Targeted Radiation Based on PET Imaging
related_trials.brief_summary: This phase III trial tests two questions by two separate comparisons of therapies. related_trials.brief_title: Abi/Pred + ADT vs ADT in PSMA-Positive, Conventionally Node-Negative Prostate Cancer
related_trials.brief_summary:
The advent of PSMA-PET has improved sensitivity and specificity in staging prostate cancer, particularly in intermediate- and high-risk disease. This has created uncertainty in the management of patients with PSMA-positive but conventionally negative pelvic lymphadenopathy (i.e., \<1 cm in smallest diameter).

This study evaluates outcomes of enhanced androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) with abiraterone and prednisone compared to standard ADT, both in combination with radiation therapy, in…

papers.title: Abiraterone acetate and prednisolone with or without enzalutamide for high-risk non-metastatic prostate cancer: a meta-analysis of primary results from two randomised controlled phase 3 trials of the STAMPEDE platform protocol
